title: Houses for Rent in Willoughby, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Willoughby, Ohio
metaDescription: Looking for houses for rent in Willoughby, Ohio? See the area's location, typical Greater Cleveland rent ranges, and how to reach our leasing team.
answerBox: Willoughby, Ohio is a Lake County suburb roughly 20 miles northeast of Cleveland, ZIP 44094, within Northeast Ohio's lake-effect snow belt. Greater Cleveland rents generally run $700–$1,800/month, though Willoughby-specific pricing isn't part of that dataset. Tell us your bedroom count and budget and we'll point you to comparable homes we currently manage.

sections.h2: Renting a house in Willoughby, Ohio
body: Willoughby is a Lake County suburb roughly 20 miles northeast of downtown Cleveland, generally reached from the city via I-90, with most addresses falling in **ZIP code 44094**. It sits near several other Lake County lakefront communities — Eastlake, Willowick, and Wickliffe are all nearby to the northwest, while Mentor, the county's largest city, is a bit farther northeast.

**Rent Finder Cleveland's 90+ managed rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods, plus a few other Ohio cities — Willoughby is not currently part of our portfolio.** If Willoughby specifically is a must, a Lake County-focused property manager or local listing service will have more direct options. If you're open to Cleveland-area neighborhoods instead, [tell our team your bedroom count and budget](/p/book-showing) and we'll show you what we currently manage.

One geographic detail worth knowing if you're weighing Willoughby against a Cleveland listing: Willoughby, like the rest of Lake County, sits within Northeast Ohio's lake-effect snow belt, which produces higher average seasonal snowfall than west-side lakefront communities. That's a function of prevailing winds off Lake Erie, not a comment on any neighborhood's desirability, but it's worth factoring into a winter commute if you're comparing the two areas.

sections.h2: What does it typically cost to rent in the Cleveland region?
body: Willoughby-specific rent data isn't published with a dated source, but as a general Northeast Ohio benchmark, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report shows a Cleveland median asking rent of **$1,250/month**, with 2-bedroom units around **$1,100** and 3-bedroom units around **$1,350**. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, which leans toward larger professionally-managed buildings, shows a higher average of **$1,564/month**. Treat both as a regional reference point rather than a Willoughby quote.

Willoughby sits outside Greater Cleveland RTA's core Cuyahoga County bus network, so a personal vehicle is the practical way to reach downtown Cleveland, generally via I-90. The commute runs through Lake County's chain of lakefront suburbs — Eastlake and Willowick sit just to the northwest, with Wickliffe a bit farther southwest toward Cuyahoga County, and Mentor extending the corridor northeast.

items: **Transit:** Outside GCRTA's core Cuyahoga County bus network; reached via I-90 by car
**Location:** Lake County, roughly 20 miles northeast of downtown Cleveland
**Climate note:** Within Northeast Ohio's lake-effect snow belt, along with the rest of Lake County

sections.h2: Section 8 and voucher holders looking at Willoughby
body: The Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA) administers the Housing Choice Voucher program for Cuyahoga County, but Willoughby is in Lake County, outside CMHA's jurisdiction — a different local public housing authority handles voucher administration there, so check with Lake County's housing authority directly for waitlist and payment-standard details. What we can confirm about our own homes: **every property Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready**, though our current inventory sits in Cleveland, not Willoughby. Our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) explains how the Cuyahoga County program generally works.

If your search spans both Lake County and Cleveland proper, our [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page covers the east and southeast side neighborhoods where we manage most of our homes. You can also [book a showing](/p/book-showing) for a comparable home near the city, or browse the full [suburb-by-suburb guide](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/) to see what's covered closer to downtown.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland manage houses in Willoughby, Ohio?
faq.a: Not currently. Our 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods and a few other Ohio cities, not Willoughby. If you're flexible on location, tell us your bedroom count and budget and we'll point you toward comparable homes we do manage.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in Willoughby, Ohio?
faq.a: There's no dated Willoughby-specific rent source, but as a regional Northeast Ohio benchmark, Zumper's July 2026 report shows a Cleveland median of about $1,100 for 2-bedroom and $1,350 for 3-bedroom units, while RentCafe's July 2026 report runs higher for larger professionally-managed buildings.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Willoughby, Ohio in?
faq.a: Willoughby is primarily ZIP code 44094, in Lake County roughly 20 miles northeast of downtown Cleveland, near Eastlake, Willowick, Wickliffe, and Mentor.

faq.q: Do Willoughby landlords accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Willoughby is in Lake County, outside CMHA's Cuyahoga County jurisdiction, so a different local housing authority handles voucher administration there — check with them directly. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, though our current homes are in Cleveland rather than Willoughby.

faq.q: Does Willoughby get more snow than Cleveland's west side?
faq.a: Generally yes. Willoughby is in Lake County, part of Northeast Ohio's lake-effect snow belt, which sees notably higher average seasonal snowfall than west-side lakefront communities. This is a geographic pattern tied to prevailing winds off Lake Erie, not a judgment about any neighborhood.
